title: Berlin Bike Lanes - Quick Summary
items: **Berlin bike lanes are traffic space, not spare pavement.** If you are walking, do not stop in a red, painted, signed or separated bike lane for photos, maps or luggage.
**Adult cyclists should not use the sidewalk unless a sign specifically allows it.** If the street feels too stressful, walk the bike or use public transport instead.
**E-scooters belong with bike traffic, not pedestrians.** Use cycle infrastructure where it exists, and do not ride or park across sidewalks, ramps, crossings or bike lanes.
**At crossings, look both ways before stepping over a bike lane.** Cyclists and scooters can appear quickly, especially on long straight streets and near stations.
**Bikes can go on parts of Berlin public transport only with space, marked carriages and the right bike ticket.** Wheelchairs and strollers have priority.
